DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT KELLY DOMER PARTNERS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Kelly Domer Partners, Inc.'s demurrer to Plaintiff Benicia Mullikin's complaint is off calendar for failure to comply with statutory meet and confer requirements. (Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 430.41.) Code of Civil Procedure section 430.41 requires that the demurring party, prior to filing the demurrer, "meet and confer in person or by telephone" with the party who filed the complaint with the purpose of attempting to "resolve the objections to be raised in the demurrer." (Id. Sec. 430.41(a).) The declaration filed by Defendant's counsel does not indicate that counsel met and conferred by phone or in person for purpose of resolving objections to be raised by demurrer. Plaintiff's counsel declares that no telephonic or in-person meet and confer occurred regarding the instant demurrer. (Kopczynski Decl. Para. 10.) Although Defendant's counsel sent three letters identifying potential defects in the complaint, he never mentioned his intention to file a demurrer on those grounds. Plaintiff's counsel indicates that when they spoke by phone on June 26, 2019 about discovery and a site inspection, Defendant's counsel did not mention that he was preparing a demurrer, which was filed nine days later. (Id. Para. 12.) Plaintiff's counsel learned of the demurrer three days after it was filed. (Id. Para. 16.) The parties are therefore ordered to meet and confer immediately. Defendant has until September 6, 2019 to respond to the complaint.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EPS)
